sections.title: Section 1 LISTENING COMPREHENSION

parts.title: Part A
parts.instruction:
Directions: In Part A you will hear short conversations between two people. After each conversation, you will hear a question about the conversation. The conversations and questions will not be repeated. After you hear a question, read the four possible answers in your test book and choose the best answer. Then, on your answer sheet, find the number of the question and fill in the space that corresponds to the letter of the answer you have chosen. 

Listen to an example. 

On the recording, you hear: 
(man) That exam was just awful. 
(woman) Oh, it could have been worse. 
(narrator) What does the woman mean? 

In your test book, you read: 
(A) The exam was really awful. 
(B) It was the worst exam she had ever seen. 
(C) It couldn't have been more difficult. 
(D) It wasn't that hard. 

You learn from the conversation that the man thought the exam was very difficult and that the woman disagreed with the man. The best answer to the question, "What does the woman mean?" is (D), "It wasn't that hard." Therefore, the correct choice is (D).

questions.passage:
M: Carla said that you were rather rude.
W: It's unfair of her to say that about me.
questions.question: WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: Carla does not live very far away.

reason.en: This option is irrelevant to the dialogue. The dialogue is about Carla's statement and the woman's reaction to it, not about Carla's proximity.
reason.id: Opsi ini tidak relevan dengan dialog. Dialog tersebut membahas pernyataan Carla dan reaksi wanita terhadapnya, bukan tentang kedekatan Carla.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 7: Listen for Negative Expressions, the woman's statement 'It's unfair of her to say that about me' directly implies that Carla's statement was unjust or unfair. The word 'unfair' is a direct response to the accusation.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 7: Listen for Negative Expressions, pernyataan wanita 'It's unfair of her to say that about me' secara langsung menyiratkan bahwa pernyataan Carla tidak adil atau tidak benar. Kata 'unfair' adalah respons langsung terhadap tuduhan tersebut.

options.text: He does not fear what anyone says.

reason.en: This option misinterprets the woman's statement. She is reacting to Carla's specific comment, not making a general statement about her own fearlessness regarding others' opinions.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah menafsirkan pernyataan wanita tersebut. Dia bereaksi terhadap komentar spesifik Carla, bukan membuat pernyataan umum tentang ketidakpeduliannya terhadap pendapat orang lain.

options.text: Carla is fairly rude to others.

reason.en: This option is the opposite of what the woman is implying. By saying Carla's statement is 'unfair,' she suggests that Carla's accusation about her rudeness is not true.
reason.id: Opsi ini adalah kebalikan dari apa yang tersirat oleh wanita tersebut. Dengan mengatakan pernyataan Carla 'tidak adil', dia menyiratkan bahwa tuduhan Carla tentang kekasarannya tidak benar.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A: Short Dialogues - Skill 7: Listen for Negative Expressions

questions.passage:
M: I don't think this painting is very good.
W: It's better than the first one, isn't it?
questions.question: WHAT DOES THE WOMAN SAY ABOUT THE PAINTING?

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms, the woman's statement 'It's better than the first one, isn't it?' implies an improvement. The correct answer paraphrases this idea using the synonym 'improvement'.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms, pernyataan wanita 'It's better than the first one, isn't it?' menyiratkan adanya peningkatan. Jawaban yang benar memparafrasekan ide ini menggunakan sinonim 'improvement'.

options.text: The fir trees in it are better.

reason.en: This is incorrect. The dialogue compares the current painting to the first one, not specifically to 'fir trees'. This option introduces irrelevant details.
reason.id: Ini salah. Dialog membandingkan lukisan saat ini dengan yang pertama, bukan secara spesifik dengan 'pohon cemara'. Opsi ini memperkenalkan detail yang tidak relevan.

reason.en: This is incorrect. The woman says the current painting is *better* than the first one, not that it *resembles* it. This option misinterprets the comparison.
reason.id: Ini salah. Wanita itu mengatakan lukisan saat ini *lebih baik* daripada yang pertama, bukan bahwa lukisan itu *menyerupai* lukisan pertama. Opsi ini salah menafsirkan perbandingan.

options.text: It is the best the man has ever done.

reason.en: This is incorrect. The dialogue does not provide information about the man's past work or suggest this is his best painting. This is an unsupported inference.
reason.id: Ini salah. Dialog tidak memberikan informasi tentang karya pria di masa lalu atau menyarankan bahwa ini adalah lukisan terbaiknya. Ini adalah kesimpulan yang tidak didukung.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A: Skill 2 - Choose Answers with Synonyms

questions.passage:
W: Your graduation ceremony is this afternoon.
M: I can't believe it. I've graduated at last!
questions.question: WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: He graduated last in his class.

reason.en: This answer is incorrect because 'last in his class' implies a low ranking, which is not suggested by the man's statement of accomplishment.
reason.id: Jawaban ini salah karena 'last in his class' menyiratkan peringkat rendah, yang tidak disarankan oleh pernyataan pencapaian pria tersebut.

options.text: He is the last person in his family to graduate.

reason.en: This answer is incorrect because the dialogue does not provide any information about the man's family or their graduation status.
reason.id: Jawaban ini salah karena dialog tidak memberikan informasi apa pun tentang keluarga pria tersebut atau status kelulusan mereka.

options.text: He doesn't believe he can improve gradually.

reason.en: This answer is incorrect because it misinterprets 'graduated at last' as 'improve gradually.' The word 'gradually' is not present in the dialogue, and the meaning is completely different.
reason.id: Jawaban ini salah karena salah menafsirkan 'graduated at last' sebagai 'improve gradually.' Kata 'gradually' tidak ada dalam dialog, dan artinya sangat berbeda.

options.text: He has finally finished his studies.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms, the man's statement 'I've graduated at last!' means he has finally finished his studies. 'At last' is a synonym for 'finally,' and 'graduated' refers to completing his studies.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms, pernyataan pria 'I've graduated at last!' berarti dia akhirnya menyelesaikan studinya. 'At last' adalah sinonim untuk 'finally,' dan 'graduated' mengacu pada penyelesaian studinya.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A: Short Dialogues (Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms)

questions.passage:
W: I got this dress for only five dollars!
M: Five dollars! How did you get it so cheap?
questions.question: WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: He's surprised there were five dresses.

reason.en: The man's exclamation 'Five dollars!' is a reaction to the price, not the quantity of dresses. This answer misinterprets the focus of the surprise.
reason.id: Seruan pria 'Five dollars!' adalah reaksi terhadap harga, bukan jumlah gaun. Jawaban ini salah menafsirkan fokus keterkejutan.

options.text: It was an unexpectedly inexpensive dress.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 13: LISTEN FOR EMPHATIC EXPRESSIONS OF SURPRISE, the man's exclamation 'Five dollars!' and his follow-up question 'How did you get it so cheap?' clearly indicate surprise at the low price. The phrase 'so cheap' implies it was unexpectedly inexpensive.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 13: LISTEN FOR EMPHATIC EXPRESSIONS OF SURPRISE, seruan pria 'Five dollars!' dan pertanyaan lanjutannya 'How did you get it so cheap?' dengan jelas menunjukkan keterkejutan pada harga yang rendah. Frasa 'so cheap' menyiratkan bahwa harganya sangat murah di luar dugaan.

options.text: He would like to know what color dress it was.

reason.en: The dialogue is about the price of the dress, not its color. The man's question is about how the woman obtained it cheaply.
reason.id: Dialog tersebut membahas tentang harga gaun, bukan warnanya. Pertanyaan pria itu adalah tentang bagaimana wanita itu mendapatkannya dengan murah.

reason.en: The man's question 'How did you get it so cheap?' directly contradicts this option. His surprise is about the low price, implying it *was* cheap.
reason.id: Pertanyaan pria 'How did you get it so cheap?' secara langsung bertentangan dengan pilihan ini. Keterkejutannya adalah tentang harga yang rendah, yang menyiratkan bahwa gaun itu *memang* murah.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A: Short Dialogues - Skill 13: LISTEN FOR EMPHATIC EXPRESSIONS OF SURPRISE

questions.passage:
W: I just got my third parking ticket this week.
M: Why don't you try putting more money in the parking meter when you park your car?
questions.question: WHAT DOES THE WOMAN SUGGEST THAT THE MAN DO?

reason.en: The man suggests a solution to the parking ticket problem, not that the woman should leave her car somewhere else.
reason.id: Pria itu menyarankan solusi untuk masalah tiket parkir, bukan agar wanita itu memarkir mobilnya di tempat lain.

reason.en: The man's suggestion is to avoid getting tickets by putting more money in the meter, not to ignore the tickets received.
reason.id: Saran pria itu adalah untuk menghindari mendapatkan tiket dengan memasukkan lebih banyak uang ke meteran, bukan untuk mengabaikan tiket yang sudah diterima.

options.text: Add more money to the meter.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 1: Focus on the Last Line, the man's suggestion to 'try putting more money in the parking meter' directly addresses how to avoid future tickets, making this the correct inference.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 1: Focus on the Last Line, saran pria itu untuk 'mencoba memasukkan lebih banyak uang ke meteran parkir' secara langsung menjawab cara menghindari tiket di masa mendatang, menjadikan ini kesimpulan yang benar.

reason.en: The dialogue does not mention paying a parking attendant; the suggestion is about interacting with the parking meter.
reason.id: Dialog tersebut tidak menyebutkan pembayaran kepada petugas parkir; saran tersebut berkaitan dengan interaksi dengan meteran parkir.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION ONE — LISTENING, Part A: Short Dialogues, Skill 1: Focus on the Last Line

questions.passage:
W: Were you able to get hold of the book that you wanted?
M: I couldn't. At the bookstore, they told me that it wasn't available yet.
questions.question: WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: He does not like to hold too many books at one time.

reason.en: This answer is incorrect because the man is talking about a specific book, not his general ability to hold books. The phrase 'get hold of' means to obtain or acquire, not to physically hold.
reason.id: Jawaban ini salah karena pria tersebut berbicara tentang buku tertentu, bukan kemampuannya secara umum untuk memegang buku. Frasa 'get hold of' berarti mendapatkan atau memperoleh, bukan memegang secara fisik.

options.text: There is no bookstore in his neighborhood.

reason.en: This answer is incorrect. The dialogue mentions 'the bookstore,' implying the existence of at least one bookstore, but it doesn't provide information about whether there are bookstores in his neighborhood or not.
reason.id: Jawaban ini salah. Dialog tersebut menyebutkan 'toko buku', menyiratkan adanya setidaknya satu toko buku, tetapi tidak memberikan informasi apakah ada toko buku di lingkungannya atau tidak.

options.text: It's not possible to obtain the book yet.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms, the correct answer is 'It's not possible to obtain the book yet.' The man says, 'I couldn't. At the bookstore, they told me that it wasn't available yet.' This directly means that he was unable to get the book because it is not yet available for purchase or acquisition.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms, jawaban yang benar adalah 'It's not possible to obtain the book yet.' Pria itu berkata, 'I couldn't. At the bookstore, they told me that it wasn't available yet.' Ini secara langsung berarti bahwa dia tidak dapat memperoleh buku tersebut karena belum tersedia untuk dibeli atau diperoleh.

options.text: He needs to talk to someone at the bookstore.

reason.en: This answer is incorrect. While the man did talk to someone at the bookstore, the core meaning of his statement is about the book's availability, not a need to further communicate with the bookstore staff. He is reporting what they told him.
reason.id: Jawaban ini salah. Meskipun pria itu berbicara dengan seseorang di toko buku, inti dari pernyataannya adalah tentang ketersediaan buku, bukan kebutuhan untuk berkomunikasi lebih lanjut dengan staf toko buku. Dia melaporkan apa yang mereka katakan kepadanya.

questions.tested_skill: LISTENING - PART A: SHORT DIALOGUES - Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms

questions.passage:
W: Professor Mitchell's lecture certainly went on and on for quite some time.
M: I thought he was never going to finish.
questions.question: WHAT DOES THE MAN IMPLY ABOUT THE LECTURE?

reason.en: The dialogue implies the lecture was long, not that it was incomplete. The phrase 'went on and on' suggests it continued for an extended period.
reason.id: Dialog menyiratkan bahwa kuliah itu panjang, bukan tidak lengkap. Frasa 'went on and on' menunjukkan bahwa kuliah itu berlanjut dalam waktu yang lama.

reason.en: This is the opposite of what the man implies. The phrase 'I thought he was never going to finish' indicates the opposite of finishing on time.
reason.id: Ini adalah kebalikan dari apa yang tersirat oleh pria itu. Frasa 'I thought he was never going to finish' menunjukkan kebalikan dari selesai tepat waktu.

reason.en: The topic of honor is not mentioned or implied in the dialogue. The conversation is about the length of the lecture.
reason.id: Topik tentang kehormatan tidak disebutkan atau tersirat dalam dialog. Percakapan ini tentang durasi kuliah.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 13: Listen for Emphatic Expressions of Surprise, the phrase 'went on and on' and 'I thought he was never going to finish' are emphatic expressions that imply the lecture was excessively long, suggesting the speaker's surprise or impatience at its duration.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 13: Listen for Emphatic Expressions of Surprise, frasa 'went on and on' dan 'I thought he was never going to finish' adalah ekspresi penekanan yang menyiratkan bahwa kuliah itu terlalu panjang, menunjukkan kejutan atau ketidaksabaran pembicara pada durasinya.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A: Short Dialogues (Skills 1-17) - Skill 13: Listen for Emphatic Expressions of Surprise

questions.passage:
M: You don't have the notes from yester day's physics class? 
W: No, I don't. Do you think I could borrow yours?
questions.question: WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: She needs to use the man's notes.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 1: Focus on the Last Line, the woman's last line, 'No, I don't. Do you think I could borrow yours?', directly indicates her need to use the man's notes because she doesn't have her own.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 1: Focus on the Last Line, baris terakhir wanita, 'No, I don't. Do you think I could borrow yours?', secara langsung menunjukkan kebutuhannya untuk menggunakan catatan pria karena dia tidak punya catatannya sendiri.

options.text: Yesterday's physics class was quite boring.

reason.en: This option is incorrect. The dialogue does not contain any information about whether the physics class was boring or not. This is a distractor.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah. Dialog tidak berisi informasi tentang apakah kelas fisika itu membosankan atau tidak. Ini adalah pengecoh.

options.text: She took some very good notes in physics class.

reason.en: This option is incorrect. The woman explicitly states she does not have the notes ('No, I don't.'), contradicting the idea that she took good notes.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah. Wanita itu secara eksplisit menyatakan dia tidak punya catatannya ('No, I don't.'), bertentangan dengan gagasan bahwa dia membuat catatan yang bagus.

options.text: She would like to lend the man her  notes.

reason.en: This option is incorrect. The woman asks to borrow the man's notes, not to lend her own. This reverses the meaning of her request.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah. Wanita itu meminta untuk meminjam catatan pria, bukan meminjamkan catatannya sendiri. Ini membalikkan makna permintaannya.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A: Skill 1 - Focus on the Last Line

questions.passage:
M: You said that you wanted to go shopping this afternoon. What do you want to get?
W: I think I'd like to get my dad a new wallet for his birthday.
questions.question: WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

reason.en: The dialogue states the wallet is for 'his dad's birthday,' not hers. This is a misinterpretation of the possessive pronoun.
reason.id: Dialog menyatakan bahwa dompet itu untuk 'ulang tahun ayahnya', bukan ulang tahunnya. Ini adalah salah tafsir dari kata ganti posesif.

options.text: She's looking for a birthday gift.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms, the woman explicitly states she wants to 'get my dad a new wallet for his birthday.' This directly implies she is looking for a birthday gift.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms, wanita itu secara eksplisit menyatakan bahwa dia ingin 'membelikan ayahnya dompet baru untuk ulang tahunnya'. Ini secara langsung menyiratkan bahwa dia sedang mencari hadiah ulang tahun.

options.text: She wants to go shopping with herdad.

reason.en: The dialogue does not mention the woman wanting to go shopping with her dad. The focus is on buying a gift for him.
reason.id: Dialog tidak menyebutkan wanita itu ingin pergi berbelanja bersama ayahnya. Fokusnya adalah membeli hadiah untuknya.

options.text: She wants a new wallet for herself.

reason.en: The dialogue clearly states the wallet is for 'his dad,' not for herself. This is a misreading of the intended recipient.
reason.id: Dialog dengan jelas menyatakan bahwa dompet itu untuk 'ayahnya', bukan untuk dirinya sendiri. Ini adalah salah baca mengenai penerima yang dituju.

questions.tested_skill: LISTENING - PART A: SHORT DIALOGUES - Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms

questions.passage:
W: You didn't go into the pool, even for a quick dip?
M: I put my big toe in and decided that the water was too cold for me.
questions.question: WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

reason.en: The man explicitly states the water was 'too cold for me,' which is the opposite of preferring cold water.
reason.id: Pria itu secara eksplisit menyatakan bahwa air 'terlalu dingin untuk saya', yang merupakan kebalikan dari lebih menyukai air dingin.

reason.en: The man mentions his 'big toe' as a point of reference for testing the water temperature, not as a reason for not entering.
reason.id: Pria itu menyebutkan 'jari kaki besarnya' sebagai titik referensi untuk menguji suhu air, bukan sebagai alasan untuk tidak masuk.

reason.en: The man's statement that the water was 'too cold' directly contradicts the idea that it felt refreshing.
reason.id: Pernyataan pria itu bahwa air 'terlalu dingin' secara langsung bertentangan dengan gagasan bahwa air itu terasa menyegarkan.

options.text: He didn't go for a swim.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 1: Focus on the Last Line, the man's last statement, 'the water was too cold for me,' implies he did not go into the pool for a swim because of the temperature. He only tested it with his toe.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 1: Focus on the Last Line, pernyataan terakhir pria itu, 'airnya terlalu dingin untuk saya,' menyiratkan bahwa dia tidak masuk ke kolam untuk berenang karena suhunya. Dia hanya mengujinya dengan jari kakinya.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A: Skill 1 - Focus on the Last Line

questions.passage:
M: Do you know where your sweater is?
W think I left it at my sister's house, but I'm not sure.
questions.question: WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: She just left her sister's house.

reason.en: The dialogue does not state when the woman was at her sister's house, only that she might have left the sweater there. This option is an assumption.
reason.id: Dialog tidak menyatakan kapan wanita itu berada di rumah saudaranya, hanya bahwa dia mungkin meninggalkan sweter di sana. Pilihan ini adalah asumsi.

options.text: Her sister is not at home.

reason.en: The dialogue does not mention the sister's presence or absence at home. This option is irrelevant to the woman's statement.
reason.id: Dialog tidak menyebutkan kehadiran atau ketidakhadiran saudara perempuan itu di rumah. Pilihan ini tidak relevan dengan pernyataan wanita itu.

options.text: She's not exactly sure where her sweater is.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 12: Listen for Expressions of Uncertainty and Suggestion, the woman uses the phrase 'I think... but I'm not sure,' which directly indicates uncertainty about the sweater's location.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 12: Listen for Expressions of Uncertainty and Suggestion, wanita itu menggunakan frasa 'Saya pikir... tapi saya tidak yakin,' yang secara langsung menunjukkan ketidakpastian tentang lokasi sweter.

options.text: She doesn't know where her sister lives.

reason.en: The dialogue implies the woman knows where her sister lives, as she mentions leaving something at her sister's house. This option is not supported by the text.
reason.id: Dialog menyiratkan bahwa wanita itu tahu di mana saudara perempuannya tinggal, karena dia menyebutkan meninggalkan sesuatu di rumah saudara perempuannya. Pilihan ini tidak didukung oleh teks.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A: Short Dialogues - Skill 12: Listen for Expressions of Uncertainty and Suggestion

questions.passage:
M: I need for you to work on these new accounting reports.
W: But I scarcely have time to finish the ones I already have.
questions.question: WHAT DOES THE WOMAN IMPLY?

options.text: She doesn't have time to complete additional reports.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms, the woman's statement 'I scarcely have time to finish the ones I already have' directly implies that she does not have time to complete additional reports. The word 'scarcely' means 'hardly' or 'almost not,' indicating a lack of time.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms, pernyataan wanita 'I scarcely have time to finish the ones I already have' secara langsung menyiratkan bahwa dia tidak punya waktu untuk menyelesaikan laporan tambahan. Kata 'scarcely' berarti 'hampir tidak' atau 'nyaris tidak', yang menunjukkan kekurangan waktu.

options.text: She cannot finish the reports that she is already working on.

reason.en: This option is too strong. The woman says she 'scarcely' has time, meaning she almost doesn't have time, but not that she absolutely cannot finish the reports she is already working on. It's a subtle but important distinction.
reason.id: Opsi ini terlalu kuat. Wanita itu mengatakan dia 'hampir tidak' punya waktu, yang berarti dia hampir tidak punya waktu, tetapi bukan berarti dia sama sekali tidak bisa menyelesaikan laporan yang sedang dikerjakannya. Ini adalah perbedaan yang halus namun penting.

options.text: She is scared of having responsibility for the reports.

reason.en: There is no indication in the dialogue that the woman is scared of responsibility. Her statement is about time management, not fear.
reason.id: Tidak ada indikasi dalam dialog bahwa wanita itu takut akan tanggung jawab. Pernyataannya adalah tentang manajemen waktu, bukan ketakutan.

options.text: It is not time for the accounting reports to be compiled.

reason.en: The dialogue is about the woman's current workload and her ability to take on more. It does not discuss the timing or appropriateness of when the accounting reports should be compiled.
reason.id: Dialog tersebut membahas beban kerja wanita saat ini dan kemampuannya untuk mengambil lebih banyak pekerjaan. Ini tidak membahas waktu atau kesesuaian kapan laporan akuntansi harus disusun.

questions.tested_skill: LISTENING - PART A: SHORT DIALOGUES - Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms

questions.passage:
W: How much longer do you think you're going to stay on that exercise machine?
M: I give up!
questions.question: WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms, the man's statement 'I give up!' is a direct paraphrase of having had enough exercise, implying he can no longer continue. Option [0] 'He's had enough exercise' accurately captures this meaning.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms, pernyataan pria 'I give up!' adalah parafrasa langsung dari sudah cukup berolahraga, menyiratkan bahwa dia tidak bisa melanjutkan lagi. Pilihan [0] 'He's had enough exercise' secara akurat menangkap makna ini.

options.text: He's going to give himself a reward for the hard work.

reason.en: This option is incorrect. The phrase 'I give up!' indicates cessation of effort, not a decision to reward oneself. It is the opposite of continuing hard work.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah. Frasa 'I give up!' menunjukkan penghentian usaha, bukan keputusan untuk memberi penghargaan pada diri sendiri. Ini adalah kebalikan dari melanjutkan kerja keras.

options.text: He's going to stay on for quite some time.

reason.en: This option is incorrect. 'I give up!' means the man is stopping, not continuing. It directly contradicts the idea of staying on for 'quite some time'.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah. 'I give up!' berarti pria itu berhenti, bukan melanjutkan. Ini secara langsung bertentangan dengan gagasan untuk bertahan 'cukup lama'.

options.text: He would like to give the woman an exercise machine as a gift.

reason.en: This option is incorrect. The dialogue is about the man's exercise, and there is no mention or implication of him wanting to give the woman an exercise machine as a gift. This is a non-sequitur.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah. Dialog tersebut adalah tentang latihan pria itu, dan tidak ada penyebutan atau implikasi bahwa dia ingin memberikan mesin olahraga kepada wanita itu sebagai hadiah. Ini adalah kesimpulan yang tidak logis.

questions.tested_skill: LISTENING - PART A: SHORT DIALOGUES - Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms

questions.passage:
W: Look at those waves coming in. They're as huge as I've ever seen them.
M: You can say that again!
questions.question: WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: He cannot see the huge waves.

reason.en: The man explicitly agrees with the woman by saying 'You can say that again!' which means he sees the waves and thinks they are huge.

reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 7: Listen for Negative Expressions, frasa 'wasn't exactly unexpected' adalah cara idiomatik untuk mengatakan bahwa kejadian tersebut telah diprediksi atau diharapkan. Pria itu mengkonfirmasi bahwa salju itu telah diantisipasi.

options.text: The exact amount of snow is unclear.

reason.en: The dialogue is about whether the snow was expected, not about the quantity of snow. The phrase 'exactly unexpected' refers to predictability, not amount.
reason.id: Dialog tersebut membahas apakah salju itu terduga, bukan tentang jumlah salju. Frasa 'exactly unexpected' mengacu pada prediktabilitas, bukan jumlah.

options.text: He expected the woman to go out in the snow.

reason.en: There is no information in the dialogue to suggest the man expected the woman to go out in the snow. His comment is solely about the predictability of the weather.
reason.id: Tidak ada informasi dalam dialog yang menunjukkan bahwa pria itu mengharapkan wanita itu keluar rumah saat bersalju. Komentarnya hanya tentang prediktabilitas cuaca.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A: Short Dialogues (Skill 7: Listen for Negative Expressions)

questions.passage:
M: How do you think you did on the literature exam that you had this morning?
W: I really wish I could take it over again.
questions.question: WHAT DOES THE WOMAN IMPLY?

options.text: She's going to take the test over again.

reason.en: The woman expresses a wish about the past ('I really wish I could take it over again'), which implies the opposite of what she said. She cannot take it over again, so this option is incorrect.
reason.id: Wanita itu mengungkapkan keinginan tentang masa lalu ('Saya sangat berharap saya bisa mengulanginya'), yang menyiratkan kebalikan dari apa yang dia katakan. Dia tidak bisa mengulanginya, jadi pilihan ini salah.

options.text: She thinks she did a good job on the exam.

reason.en: The phrase 'I really wish I could take it over again' directly contradicts the idea that she thinks she did a good job. If she had done well, she would not wish to retake it.
reason.id: Frasa 'Saya sangat berharap saya bisa mengulanginya' secara langsung bertentangan dengan gagasan bahwa dia berpikir dia melakukannya dengan baik. Jika dia melakukannya dengan baik, dia tidak akan berharap untuk mengulanginya.

options.text: She has not yet taken the literature exam.

reason.en: The dialogue clearly states that the exam happened 'this morning,' indicating it has already taken place. Therefore, she has already taken the exam.
reason.id: Dialog tersebut dengan jelas menyatakan bahwa ujian itu terjadi 'pagi ini', yang menunjukkan bahwa ujian itu sudah berlangsung. Oleh karena itu, dia sudah mengikuti ujian.

options.text: She's unhappy with how she did.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 14: Wishes and Unreal Conditionals, the statement 'I really wish I could take it over again' implies that the opposite is true. The opposite of wanting to take it over again is being unhappy with how she performed.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 14: Wishes and Unreal Conditionals, pernyataan 'Saya sangat berharap saya bisa mengulanginya' menyiratkan bahwa kebalikannya adalah benar. Kebalikan dari keinginan untuk mengulanginya adalah ketidakpuasan dengan kinerjanya.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A: Skills 14–15 — Contrary Meanings (Wishes & Untrue Conditions)

questions.passage:
W: You didn't have to wait outside. You could've just opened the door and walked right in.
M: So the door was not locked!
questions.question: WHAT HAD THEMAN ASSUMED?

reason.en: This is incorrect. The man's statement 'So the door was not locked!' implies he assumed the opposite, that it *was* locked.
reason.id: Ini salah. Pernyataan pria 'Jadi pintunya tidak terkunci!' menyiratkan dia berasumsi sebaliknya, yaitu pintunya *terkunci*.

options.text: It was better to wait outside.

reason.en: This is incorrect. The woman's statement implies waiting outside was unnecessary, not that it was better.
reason.id: Ini salah. Pernyataan wanita menyiratkan menunggu di luar tidak perlu, bukan lebih baik.

options.text: He could not open the door.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 7: LISTEN FOR NEGATIVE EXPRESSIONS, the woman's statement 'You didn't have to wait outside. You could've just opened the door and walked right in' implies that the man *did* wait outside, and his subsequent statement 'So the door was not locked!' reveals his assumption that the door was locked, preventing him from entering directly.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 7: LISTEN FOR NEGATIVE EXPRESSIONS, pernyataan wanita 'Kamu tidak perlu menunggu di luar. Kamu bisa saja membuka pintu dan langsung masuk' menyiratkan bahwa pria itu *memang* menunggu di luar, dan pernyataannya selanjutnya 'Jadi pintunya tidak terkunci!' mengungkapkan asumsinya bahwa pintu itu terkunci, mencegahnya masuk langsung.

options.text: He needed to take a walk.

reason.en: This is incorrect. There is no mention or implication that the man needed to take a walk.
reason.id: Ini salah. Tidak ada penyebutan atau implikasi bahwa pria itu perlu berjalan-jalan.

questions.tested_skill: LISTENING - PART A: SHORT DIALOGUES - Skill 7: LISTEN FOR NEGATIVE EXPRESSIONS

questions.passage:
M: My guess is that you're leaving the office now and heading straight home.
W: You've hit the nail on the head!
questions.question: WHAT DOES THE WOMAN SAY ABOUT THE MAN?

reason.en: This answer is incorrect because it takes the idiom 'hit the nail on the head' literally, which is a common trap for idioms. The idiom means to be exactly right, not to physically hit something.
reason.id: Jawaban ini salah karena mengartikan idiom 'hit the nail on the head' secara harfiah, yang merupakan jebakan umum untuk idiom. Idiom tersebut berarti benar sekali, bukan memukul sesuatu secara fisik.

reason.en: This answer is partially correct in that the man is indeed heading home, as confirmed by the woman. However, the question asks what the woman says ABOUT the man, and her response 'You've hit the nail on the head!' is a direct comment on the accuracy of his guess, not just a confirmation of his action.
reason.id: Jawaban ini sebagian benar karena pria itu memang menuju rumah, seperti yang dikonfirmasi oleh wanita itu. Namun, pertanyaan tersebut menanyakan apa yang dikatakan wanita itu TENTANG pria itu, dan responsnya 'You've hit the nail on the head!' adalah komentar langsung tentang keakuratan tebakannya, bukan sekadar konfirmasi tindakannya.

options.text: He hit himself in the head.

reason.en: This answer is incorrect because it takes the idiom 'hit the nail on the head' literally and incorrectly interprets the action. The idiom is figurative and does not involve self-harm.
reason.id: Jawaban ini salah karena mengartikan idiom 'hit the nail on the head' secara harfiah dan salah menafsirkan tindakan tersebut. Idiom tersebut bersifat kiasan dan tidak melibatkan menyakiti diri sendiri.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 16-17: Two/Three-Part Verbs and Idioms, the woman uses the idiom 'hit the nail on the head' to express that the man's guess was exactly correct. This is the most accurate interpretation of her statement.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 16-17: Two/Three-Part Verbs and Idioms, wanita itu menggunakan idiom 'hit the nail on the head' untuk menyatakan bahwa tebakan pria itu benar sekali. Ini adalah interpretasi yang paling akurat dari pernyataannya.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A: Short Dialogues (Skill 16-17: Two/Three-Part Verbs and Idioms)

parts.title: Part B
parts.instruction:
Directions: In this part of the test, you will hear longer conversations. After each conversation, you 
will hear several questions. The conversations and questions will not be repeated. 
After you hear a question, read the four possible answers in your test book and choose the best 
answer. Then, on your answer sheet, find the number of the question and fill in the space that 
corresponds to the letter of the answer you have chosen. 
Remember, you are not allowed to take notes or write in your test book.

questions.question: 31. WHAT SEEMS TO BE TRUE ABOUT BOB'S HAIRCUT?

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 1: Focus on the Last Line, the man's statement 'It's a lot shorter. When I look in the mirror, I don't even know who is looking back at me' directly implies that the haircut is unusually short, and he is unhappy with it. This aligns with the woman's initial observation that it's 'a bit shorter than usual'.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 1: Focus on the Last Line, pernyataan pria 'It's a lot shorter. When I look in the mirror, I don't even know who is looking back at me' secara langsung menyiratkan bahwa potongan rambutnya sangat pendek, dan dia tidak senang dengan itu. Hal ini sejalan dengan pengamatan awal wanita bahwa rambutnya 'sedikit lebih pendek dari biasanya'.

reason.en: The dialogue does not provide any information about whether this is Bob's first haircut. The focus is on the current haircut being too short.
reason.id: Dialog tidak memberikan informasi apa pun tentang apakah ini adalah potongan rambut pertama Bob. Fokusnya adalah pada potongan rambut saat ini yang terlalu pendek.

options.text: Bob doesn't know who gave him the haircut

reason.en: The man expresses shock and unfamiliarity with his own appearance after the haircut ('I don't even know who is looking back at me'), but he does not state that he doesn't know who gave him the haircut. He mentions 'the hairstylist' in the last line.
reason.id: Pria tersebut mengungkapkan keterkejutan dan ketidakbiasaan dengan penampilannya sendiri setelah potong rambut ('Saya bahkan tidak tahu siapa yang melihat kembali pada saya'), tetapi dia tidak menyatakan bahwa dia tidak tahu siapa yang memotong rambutnya. Dia menyebutkan 'penata rambut' di baris terakhir.

options.text: After the haircut, Bob's hair still touches the floor.

reason.en: The dialogue focuses on the length of the hair being cut too short, not on whether it still touches the floor. This option is an irrelevant detail not supported by the conversation.
reason.id: Dialog berfokus pada panjang rambut yang terpotong terlalu pendek, bukan pada apakah rambutnya masih menyentuh lantai. Pilihan ini adalah detail yang tidak relevan yang tidak didukung oleh percakapan.

questions.tested_skill: LISTENING - PART A: SHORT DIALOGUES - Skill 1: Focus on the Last Line

questions.question: 32. HOW DOES BOB SEEM TO FEEL ABOUT HIS HAIRCUT?

options.text: It is just what he wanted.

reason.en: The man explicitly states that the haircut is 'not even close to the haircut that I wanted,' which directly contradicts this option.
reason.id: Pria tersebut secara eksplisit menyatakan bahwa potongan rambutnya 'bahkan tidak mendekati potongan rambut yang saya inginkan', yang secara langsung bertentangan dengan pilihan ini.

options.text: He enjoys having the latest style.

reason.en: There is no mention of style or trends in the dialogue. The man's reaction is one of shock and disappointment, not enthusiasm for a new style.
reason.id: Tidak ada penyebutan gaya atau tren dalam dialog. Reaksi pria tersebut adalah keterkejutan dan kekecewaan, bukan antusiasme untuk gaya baru.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 13: LISTEN FOR EMPHATIC EXPRESSIONS OF SURPRISE, the man's statement 'I don't even know who is looking back at me' and his description of the stylist 'really went to town' when he asked for 'just a little bit' indicate strong dissatisfaction and surprise at how much hair was cut. This implies he dislikes it immensely.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 13: LISTEN FOR EMPHATIC EXPRESSIONS OF SURPRISE, pernyataan pria tersebut 'Saya bahkan tidak tahu siapa yang melihat kembali pada saya' dan deskripsinya tentang penata rambut yang 'benar-benar berlebihan' ketika dia meminta 'sedikit saja' menunjukkan ketidakpuasan yang kuat dan keterkejutan atas banyaknya rambut yang dipotong. Ini menyiratkan bahwa dia sangat tidak menyukainya.

options.text: He thinks it will be cool in the  summer.

reason.en: The dialogue focuses on the immediate dissatisfaction with the haircut. There is no discussion or implication about how the haircut might feel or look in the summer.
reason.id: Dialog tersebut berfokus pada ketidakpuasan langsung dengan potongan rambut. Tidak ada diskusi atau implikasi tentang bagaimana potongan rambut tersebut mungkin terasa atau terlihat di musim panas.

questions.tested_skill: LISTENING - PART A: SHORT DIALOGUES - Skill 13: LISTEN FOR EMPHATIC EXPRESSIONS OF SURPRISE

questions.question: 33. WHAT DID BOB SEE ON THE FLOOR?

reason.en: The dialogue does not mention a broken mirror. This is an irrelevant detail.

reason.en: The dialogue focuses on Bob's dissatisfaction with his haircut, not on the appearance of the hairstylist.
reason.id: Dialog berfokus pada ketidakpuasan Bob dengan potongan rambutnya, bukan pada penampilan penata rambut.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 4: Draw Conclusions About Who, What, Where, Bob's statement 'When I look in the mirror, I don't even know who is looking back at me' and his complaint that the stylist 'really went to town' implies that a significant amount of hair was cut off and would likely be on the floor.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 4: Draw Conclusions About Who, What, Where, pernyataan Bob 'Ketika saya melihat ke cermin, saya bahkan tidak tahu siapa yang melihat kembali pada saya' dan keluhannya bahwa penata rambut 'benar-benar beraksi' menyiratkan bahwa sejumlah besar rambut telah dipotong dan kemungkinan besar ada di lantai.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION ONE — LISTENING, Part A: Short Dialogues, Skill 4: Draw Conclusions About Who, What, Where

questions.question: 34. WHAT DO PEOPLE KEEP SAYING TO BOB?

reason.en: The dialogue does not contain any mention of Bob becoming a hairstylist or any advice related to that profession. This is an irrelevant suggestion.
reason.id: Dialog tersebut tidak mengandung penyebutan Bob menjadi penata rambut atau saran terkait profesi tersebut. Ini adalah saran yang tidak relevan.

reason.en: The dialogue is about Bob's haircut being too short. The phrase 'put it back on' is nonsensical in this context and does not reflect anything said or implied.
reason.id: Dialog tersebut tentang potongan rambut Bob yang terlalu pendek. Frasa 'memasangnya kembali' tidak masuk akal dalam konteks ini dan tidak mencerminkan apa pun yang dikatakan atau tersirat.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 13: LISTEN FOR EMPHATIC EXPRESSIONS OF SURPRISE, the man's strong negative reaction to his haircut ('I don't even know who is looking back at me,' 'This is not even close...') implies that he is unhappy with how short it is. The common comforting phrase in such situations, which people would likely say to him, is 'It'll grow back.' This is an inference based on the man's distress and the typical social response.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 13: LISTEN FOR EMPHATIC EXPRESSIONS OF SURPRISE, reaksi negatif kuat pria tersebut terhadap potongan rambutnya ('Saya bahkan tidak tahu siapa yang melihat kembali pada saya,' 'Ini bahkan tidak mendekati...') menyiratkan bahwa dia tidak senang dengan betapa pendeknya rambutnya. Ungkapan penghiburan yang umum dalam situasi seperti itu, yang kemungkinan akan dikatakan orang kepadanya, adalah 'Rambutnya akan tumbuh kembali.' Ini adalah kesimpulan berdasarkan kesusahan pria itu dan respons sosial yang biasa.

reason.en: While Bob is unhappy with the length, the dialogue doesn't explicitly state that people are telling him it won't grow fast enough. The implication of his distress is that it's too short *now*, and the common response would be about it growing back eventually.
reason.id: Meskipun Bob tidak senang dengan panjangnya, dialog tersebut tidak secara eksplisit menyatakan bahwa orang-orang memberitahunya bahwa rambutnya tidak akan tumbuh cukup cepat. Implikasi dari kesusahannya adalah rambutnya terlalu pendek *sekarang*, dan tanggapan umum adalah tentang rambut yang akan tumbuh kembali pada akhirnya.

questions.question: 36. WHAT DID THE MAN'S GREAT-GRANDMOTHER TELL HIM ON THE PHONE THIS MORNING?

options.text: That she was eighty-five years old

reason.en: The man's great-grandmother's age (over eighty-five) is mentioned by the man, not directly stated by her to him on the phone this morning.
reason.id: Usia nenek buyut pria tersebut (lebih dari delapan puluh lima) disebutkan oleh pria itu, bukan dinyatakan langsung olehnya kepada pria itu di telepon pagi ini.

reason.en: Based on Listening Skill 1: Focus on the Last Line, the man explicitly states, 'She told me to be careful because a big storm is coming.' This directly answers the question about what his great-grandmother told him.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Listening Skill 1: Focus on the Last Line, pria itu secara eksplisit menyatakan, 'Dia menyuruhku untuk berhati-hati karena badai besar akan datang.' Ini secara langsung menjawab pertanyaan tentang apa yang dikatakan nenek buyutnya kepadanya.

options.text: That she was under a great deal of pressure

reason.en: The great-grandmother mentions feeling the storm 'in her bones,' which the woman explains is due to pressure in her joints. However, the great-grandmother herself does not state she is 'under a great deal of pressure.'
reason.id: Nenek buyut menyebutkan merasakan badai 'di tulangnya', yang dijelaskan oleh wanita itu karena tekanan di persendiannya. Namun, nenek buyut itu sendiri tidak menyatakan bahwa dia 'berada di bawah banyak tekanan'.

options.text: That she wanted to become a weather forecaster

reason.en: The woman asks if the great-grandmother is a weather forecaster, but there is no indication that she wants to become one. Her ability to predict storms is presented as a physical sensation.
reason.id: Wanita itu bertanya apakah nenek buyut adalah peramal cuaca, tetapi tidak ada indikasi bahwa dia ingin menjadi salah satunya. Kemampuannya memprediksi badai disajikan sebagai sensasi fisik.

groups.label: Q 31-34

groups.passage:
(narrator) Listen to a conversation about a man's great-grandmother. 
(man) I talked to my great-grandmother on the phone this morning. 
(woman) Your great-grandmother? Do you talk with her often? 
(man) I try to call her at least once a week. She's a really wonderful woman, and she's over eighty-five years old. I enjoy talking to her, because she's so understanding and because she gives me 
good advice. 
(woman) What advice did she have for you today? 
(man) (laughs) She told me to be careful because a big storm is coming.  
(woman) She said that a big storm is coming? Is she a weather forecaster? 
(man) Not exactly. She says that she can feel it in her bones when a storm is coming. I know it sounds funny, but when she feels it in her bones that a storm is coming, she's usually right. 
(woman) That's not actually so funny. When people get older, the tissue around their joints can become stiff and swollen. Just before a storm, the air pressure often drops, and this drop in air pressure can cause additional pressure and pain in swollen joints. So when your great-grandmother tells you she thinks a storm is coming, she probably has some aching in her joints from the decreasing air pressure. 
(man) Then, I had better pay more attention to my great-grandmother's weather forecasts!

groups.passage:
Questions 39-42 
(narrator) Listen to a talk by a tour guide in the Everglades National Park. 
(man) Today we're going to be taking a tram tour through part of the Everglades National Park. Quite probably we'll be seeing a number of crocodiles sunning themselves by the side of the water or poking their heads up through the water. Needless to say, we will not be getting off the tram at any time until we leave the area because of the danger posed by the crocodiles. 

By the way, you've probably heard of the expression "crying crocodile tears." It is common to say that someone is crying crocodile tears when he or she is pretending to be sad or full of regret. Crocodiles always appear to have tears in their eyes, but they are not crying because of sadness, or even pretended sadness. Instead, a crocodile uses its tear ducts to get rid of extra salt from its body. A crocodile 
does not sweat the same way that humans do and must get rid of extra salt through tears. So if you see a crying crocodile, do not think that it's feeling sad; it is basically sweating through its eyes. 

Look! Over there on the right. There are two large crocodiles on the water's edge, right next to the fallen trees. You can get out your cameras and take pictures from here on the tram, but no, you cannot get off the tram to get any closer.
groups.label: Q39-42

groups.passage:
(narrator) Listen to the following lecture by a 
university professor. 
(woman) Please take your seats now because I would like to begin today's lecture. Today, we will be discussing one of the more elegant and distinct forms of nineteenth-century transportation—the clipper ship. 

Clipper ships of the nineteenth century were the graceful, multisailed, oceangoing vessels that were designed for maximum speed. They were given the name "clipper" ship in reference to the fact that they "clipped along" at such a fast rate of speed. 

Clipper ships were constructed with a large number of sails in order to maximize their speed. They often had six to eight sails on each of the masts, and ships commonly had three and 
perhaps four masts. The speeds that they achieved were unbelievably fast for the era; clipper ships could, for example, accomplish the amazing feat of traveling from New York to San Francisco in less than a hundred days.

Clipper ships first came into use in the United States in the 1840s. They were originally intended to make the trip from New York, around the tip of South America, and on to China in order to transport tea to the United States. Once gold was discovered in California in 1848, clipper ships were immediately put into use to carry large numbers of gold prospectors and large amounts of mining supplies from the East Coast to California. With the success of the American clipper ships, the British began their own fleet of clipper ships to transport goods from the far reaches of the 
British Empire.
groups.label: Q43-46

groups.passage:
(narrator) Listen to the following talk by a drama coach to a group of actors. 

(man) I know that some of you are feeling more than a little nervous about tonight's performance, and I want you to understand that this is quite a natural feeling. You are going to be on a stage in front of a lot of people tonight, and it's normal to be experiencing some nerves. I would like to help you to understand these feelings and not to let them interfere with your performance. 

What you are experiencing is called stage fright. Stage fright is the fear that develops before you give a performance in front of an audience. Stage fright is not just experienced by actors and actresses; it can also be experienced by musicians, athletes, teachers—anyone who performs in front of a group of people. It occurs before a performance when a performer is concerned about looking foolish in front of others. Just before tonight's performance, if you are feeling a bit tense, if your knees are shaking, if your stomach has butterflies in it, and if you are thinking about how bad your performance could be, then you have a major case of stage fright. 

To control stage fright, you can work to control both the physical reactions and the negative thoughts. To combat the physical reactions, you can try techniques such as deep breathing, muscle relaxation, or even just laughing to relieve some of the pressure. To combat the negative thoughts, you should force yourself to focus on what you have to do rather than on what other people are going to think. 

That's all I have to say for now. I'll see you back here at six o'clock because the performance starts at eight o'clock. Just remember that if you begin to feel at all nervous, try some deep breathing to relax and focus your thoughts on the performance that you are about to give. See you his evening.
groups.label: Q47-50

sections.instruction:
Time—approximately 35 minutes 
(including the reading of the directions for each part) 

In this section of the test, you will have an opportunity to demonstrate your ability to understand conversations and talks in English. There are three parts to this section. Answer all the questions on the basis of what is stated or implied by the speakers you hear. Do not take notes or write in your test book at any time. Do not turn the pages until you are told to do so.

parts.title: Structure
parts.instruction: Questions 1-15 are incomplete sentences. Beneath each sentence you will see four words or phrases, marked (A), (B), (C), and (D). Choose the one word or phrase that best completes the sentence. Then, on your answer sheet, find the number of the question and fill in the space that corresponds to the letter of the answer you have chosen. Fill in the space so that the letter inside the oval cannot be seen.

questions.question: Q: The compound microscope has not one _________ two lenses.

reason.en: This option is incorrect because 'and also' is redundant and does not create a grammatically correct parallel structure when connecting two contrasting elements. The sentence structure 'not one X and also Y' is awkward and incorrect.

reason.en: This is the correct answer. The structure 'not only... but also...' is a common correlative conjunction pair. However, in this specific sentence, the structure is 'not one X... but Y'. The word 'but' correctly connects the two contrasting elements, indicating that instead of having one lens, the microscope has two. This aligns with the principle of connecting clauses or phrases correctly, often using conjunctions like 'but' to show contrast.

Kata 'but' secara tepat menghubungkan dua elemen yang kontras, menunjukkan bahwa alih-alih memiliki satu lensa, mikroskop memiliki dua. Ini sejalan dengan prinsip menghubungkan klausa atau frasa dengan benar, seringkali menggunakan konjungsi seperti 'but' untuk menunjukkan kontras.

reason.en: This option is incorrect because 'and there are' introduces a new independent clause, but the sentence structure requires a conjunction to connect the two parts of the phrase 'not one... two lenses'. It creates a run-on sentence and does not fit the intended meaning of contrast.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah karena 'and there are' memperkenalkan klausa independen baru, tetapi struktur kalimat memerlukan konjungsi untuk menghubungkan kedua bagian dari frasa 'not one... two lenses'. Ini menciptakan kalimat yang berlarut-larut dan tidak sesuai dengan makna kontras yang dimaksud.

reason.en: This option is incorrect because 'but there are' introduces a new independent clause. While 'but' indicates contrast, the addition of 'there are' makes the sentence grammatically incorrect and unnecessarily wordy for this structure. The sentence needs a simple conjunction to link the two contrasting elements, not a new clause.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah karena 'but there are' memperkenalkan klausa independen baru. Meskipun 'but' menunjukkan kontras, penambahan 'there are' membuat kalimat tersebut salah secara tata bahasa dan terlalu bertele-tele untuk struktur ini. Sebagai klausa yang direduksi, hanya past participle yang diperlukan untuk memodifikasi 'stock'.

questions.question: Q: The leaves of the white mulberry provide food for silkworms, ________  silk fabrics are woven.

reason.en: This option is incorrect because 'whose cocoons' is a noun phrase and cannot function as a connector to introduce a clause that modifies 'silk fabrics'. The sentence requires a connector that links the origin of the silk (cocoons) to the silk fabrics.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah karena 'whose cocoons' adalah frasa kata benda dan tidak dapat berfungsi sebagai penghubung untuk memperkenalkan klausa yang memodifikasi 'silk fabrics'. Kalimat tersebut membutuhkan penghubung yang menghubungkan asal sutra (kepompong) dengan kain sutra.

reason.en: This option is incorrect because 'from cocoons' is a prepositional phrase and cannot introduce an adjective clause to modify 'silk fabrics'. It lacks a relative pronoun to connect it to the noun it modifies.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah karena 'from cocoons' adalah frasa preposisional dan tidak dapat memperkenalkan klausa adjektiva untuk memodifikasi 'silk fabrics'. Frasa ini tidak memiliki kata ganti relatif untuk menghubungkannya dengan kata benda yang dimodifikasinya.

reason.en: This option is grammatically awkward and incorrect. 'Whose cocoons are from' does not form a proper adjective clause to modify 'silk fabrics'. The structure is convoluted and does not clearly indicate the relationship between the cocoons and the silk fabrics.
reason.id: Opsi ini secara tata bahasa canggung dan salah. 'Whose cocoons are from' tidak membentuk klausa adjektiva yang tepat untuk memodifikasi 'silk fabrics'. Strukturnya berbelit-belit dan tidak secara jelas menunjukkan hubungan antara kepompong dan kain sutra.

reason.en: Based on Structure Skill 9-12: Noun and Adjective Clause Connectors, this is the correct answer. 'From whose cocoons' functions as a relative adverbial phrase within an adjective clause modifying 'silk fabrics'. It correctly indicates that the silk fabrics are made from the cocoons of silkworms, establishing the necessary relationship and grammatical structure.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Structure Skill 9-12: Noun and Adjective Clause Connectors, ini adalah jawaban yang benar. 'From whose cocoons' berfungsi sebagai frasa adverbial relatif dalam klausa adjektiva yang memodifikasi 'silk fabrics'. Frasa ini secara tepat menunjukkan bahwa kain sutra dibuat dari kepompong ulat sutra, menetapkan hubungan dan struktur tata bahasa yang diperlukan.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION TWO — STRUCTURE & WRITTEN EXPRESSION - Skill 9–12 — Noun and Adjective Clause Connectors

questions.question: Q: Not only __________ generate energy, but it also produces fuel for other fission reactors.

reason.en: This option creates a sentence fragment. The phrase 'a nuclear breeder reactor' lacks a verb and cannot stand alone after the introductory phrase 'Not only...'. The structure requires inversion after 'Not only'.
reason.id: Opsi ini menciptakan fragmen kalimat. Frasa 'a nuclear breeder reactor' tidak memiliki kata kerja dan tidak dapat berdiri sendiri setelah frasa pengantar 'Not only...'. Struktur ini memerlukan inversi setelah 'Not only'.

options.text: it is a nuclear breeder reactor

reason.en: This option does not correctly invert the subject and verb after the negative introductory phrase 'Not only'. The correct structure requires the auxiliary verb to come before the subject.
reason.id: Opsi ini tidak membalikkan subjek dan kata kerja dengan benar setelah frasa pengantar negatif 'Not only'. Struktur yang benar memerlukan kata kerja bantu untuk mendahului subjek.

reason.en: Based on Structure Skill 15: Subject/Verb Inversion, the phrase 'Not only' at the beginning of a sentence triggers subject-verb inversion. The structure should be 'Not only + auxiliary verb + subject + main verb'. Here, 'does' is the auxiliary verb, 'a nuclear breeder reactor' is the subject, and 'generate' is the main verb.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Structure Skill 15: Subject/Verb Inversion, frasa 'Not only' di awal kalimat memicu inversi subjek-kata kerja. Strukturnya seharusnya 'Not only + kata kerja bantu + subjek + kata kerja utama'. Di sini, 'does' adalah kata kerja bantu, 'a nuclear breeder reactor' adalah subjek, dan 'generate' adalah kata kerja utama.

reason.en: This option incorrectly places the subject after the main verb 'generate' instead of the auxiliary verb. The structure 'Not only + auxiliary verb + subject + main verb' requires the auxiliary verb to precede the subject.
reason.id: Opsi ini secara keliru menempatkan subjek setelah kata kerja utama 'generate' alih-alih kata kerja bantu. Struktur 'Not only + kata kerja bantu + subjek + kata kerja utama' mengharuskan kata kerja bantu mendahului subjek.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION TWO — STRUCTURE & WRITTEN EXPRESSION - Skills 15–19 — Subject/Verb Inversion

questions.question: Q: D.W. Griffith pioneered many of the stylistic features and filmmaking techniques _______  as the Hollywood standard.

reason.en: This option uses 'that established,' which creates an adjective clause. However, the verb 'established' is in the active voice, implying that the features and techniques themselves established the Hollywood standard, which is illogical. The features and techniques were what *became* established as the standard.
reason.id: Opsi ini menggunakan 'that established', yang menciptakan klausa adjektiva. Namun, kata kerja 'established' dalam bentuk aktif, menyiratkan bahwa fitur dan teknik itu sendiri yang menetapkan standar Hollywood, yang tidak logis. Fitur dan teknik itulah yang *menjadi* mapan sebagai standar.

reason.en: Based on Structure Skill 13: Reduced Clauses (and general clause structure), 'that became established' correctly forms a reduced adjective clause modifying 'features and filmmaking techniques.' The relative pronoun 'that' is followed by a verb phrase in the passive voice ('became established'), indicating that the techniques were the subject of the establishment of the Hollywood standard. This is the most logical and grammaturally correct completion.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Structure Skill 13: Reduced Clauses (dan struktur klausa umum), 'that became established' secara benar membentuk klausa adjektiva yang direduksi yang memodifikasi 'features and filmmaking techniques'. Kata ganti relatif 'that' diikuti oleh frasa kata kerja dalam bentuk pasif ('became established'), menunjukkan bahwa teknik-teknik tersebut adalah subjek dari penetapan standar Hollywood. Ini adalah penyelesaian yang paling logis dan benar secara tata bahasa.

reason.en: This option uses 'what established.' 'What' is a noun clause connector and cannot introduce an adjective clause modifying a noun like 'features and filmmaking techniques.' Furthermore, similar to option 0, the active verb 'established' is illogical in this context.
reason.id: Opsi ini menggunakan 'what established'. 'What' adalah konektor klausa nomina dan tidak dapat memperkenalkan klausa adjektiva yang memodifikasi kata benda seperti 'features and filmmaking techniques'. Selain itu, mirip dengan opsi 0, kata kerja aktif 'established' tidak logis dalam konteks ini.

reason.en: This option uses 'what became established.' While 'became established' is the correct verb phrase, 'what' is a noun clause connector and cannot function as a relative pronoun to introduce an adjective clause modifying 'features and filmmaking techniques.' The sentence requires a relative pronoun like 'that' or 'which' to connect the clause to the noun it modifies.
reason.id: Opsi ini menggunakan 'what became established'. Meskipun 'became established' adalah frasa kata kerja yang benar, 'what' adalah konektor klausa nomina dan tidak dapat berfungsi sebagai kata ganti relatif untuk memperkenalkan klausa adjektiva yang memodifikasi 'features and filmmaking techniques'. Kalimat tersebut memerlukan kata ganti relatif seperti 'that' atau 'which' untuk menghubungkan klausa ke kata benda yang dimodifikasinya.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION TWO — STRUCTURE & WRITTEN EXPRESSION: Skills 9–12 — Noun and Adjective Clause Connectors

parts.title: Written Expression
parts.instruction: In questions 16-40, each sentence has four underlined words or phrases. The four underlined parts of the sentence are marked (A), (B), (C), and (D). Identify the one underlined word or phrase that must be changed in order for the sentence to be correct. Then, on your answer sheet, find the number of the question and fill in the space that corresponds to the letter of the answer you have chosen.

questions.question: Q: Mosquitoes will ~[accepts]~(A) the ~[malaria]~(B) parasite at ~[only one]~(C) stage of the parasite's ~[complex]~(D) life cycle.

reason.en: Based on Reading Skill 11: Use Context for Simple Words, the word 'accepts' is the correct choice because the context of the sentence implies that mosquitoes take in or receive the malaria parasite at a specific stage. The other options are incorrect because 'malaria' is the disease, 'only one' refers to the stage, and 'complex' describes the life cycle, none of which fit grammatically or contextually as the verb.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Reading Skill 11: Use Context for Simple Words, kata 'accepts' adalah pilihan yang tepat karena konteks kalimat menyiratkan bahwa nyamuk menerima atau mengambil parasit malaria pada tahap tertentu. Pilihan lainnya salah karena 'malaria' adalah penyakitnya, 'only one' merujuk pada tahapnya, dan 'complex' menggambarkan siklus hidupnya, yang tidak ada yang cocok secara tata bahasa atau kontekstual sebagai kata kerja.

reason.en: The word 'malaria' is a noun referring to the disease, not a verb that fits the blank. The sentence requires a verb to describe the action of the mosquito in relation to the parasite.
reason.id: Kata 'malaria' adalah kata benda yang merujuk pada penyakit, bukan kata kerja yang cocok untuk mengisi bagian yang kosong. Kalimat tersebut membutuhkan kata kerja untuk menggambarkan tindakan nyamuk terkait parasit.

reason.en: The phrase 'only one' is an adjective phrase describing the stage, not a verb that fits the blank. The sentence structure requires a verb here.
reason.id: Frasa 'only one' adalah frasa kata sifat yang menggambarkan tahapnya, bukan kata kerja yang cocok untuk mengisi bagian yang kosong. Struktur kalimat membutuhkan kata kerja di sini.

reason.en: The word 'complex' is an adjective describing the life cycle, not a verb that fits the blank. The sentence requires a verb to describe the action of the mosquito.
reason.id: Kata 'complex' adalah kata sifat yang menggambarkan siklus hidup, bukan kata kerja yang cocok untuk mengisi bagian yang kosong. Kalimat tersebut membutuhkan kata kerja untuk menggambarkan tindakan nyamuk.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION THREE — READING COMPREHENSION: Skill 11 — Use Context for Simple Words

questions.question: Q: The ~[counterpart]~(A) ~[of]~(B) a negative ~[electrons]~(C) is the ~[positive]~(D) proton.

reason.en: This word is a noun and fits grammatically and contextually as the subject complement.
reason.id: Kata ini adalah kata benda dan cocok secara tata bahasa dan kontekstual sebagai pelengkap subjek.

reason.en: This preposition is grammatically correct in this context.
reason.id: Preposisi ini secara tata bahasa benar dalam konteks ini.

reason.en: Based on Structure Skill 5: Be Careful of Present and Past Participles, and the general understanding of sentence structure, the sentence requires a subject and a verb to form a complete thought. The word 'electrons' is a noun, but the sentence structure implies a need for a subject that can be described by 'positive'. In a scientific context, 'proton' is the positively charged particle that is the counterpart to the negatively charged electron. Therefore, 'electrons' is the incorrect word choice as it does not fit the intended meaning or grammatical role in this specific sentence structure, which is likely testing the understanding of scientific terms and their relationship.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Structure Skill 5: Be Careful of Present and Past Participles, dan pemahaman umum tentang struktur kalimat, kalimat ini membutuhkan subjek dan kata kerja untuk membentuk pemikiran yang lengkap. Kata 'electrons' adalah kata benda, tetapi struktur kalimat menyiratkan kebutuhan akan subjek yang dapat digambarkan oleh 'positive'. Dalam konteks ilmiah, 'proton' adalah partikel bermuatan positif yang merupakan tandingan dari elektron bermuatan negatif. Oleh karena itu, 'electrons' adalah pilihan kata yang salah karena tidak sesuai dengan makna yang dimaksud atau peran tata bahasa dalam struktur kalimat spesifik ini, yang kemungkinan menguji pemahaman istilah ilmiah dan hubungannya.

reason.en: This word is an adjective and fits grammatically and contextually to describe the noun 'proton'.
reason.id: Kata ini adalah kata sifat dan cocok secara tata bahasa dan kontekstual untuk menggambarkan kata benda 'proton'.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION TWO — STRUCTURE & WRITTEN EXPRESSION - Skills 43–45 — Pronouns

questions.question: Q: The ankle joint ~[occur]~(A) where the ~[lower]~(B) ends of the tibia and fibula slot ~[neatly]~(C) ~[around]~(D) the talus.

reason.en: Based on Structure Skill 1: Be Sure the Sentence Has a Subject and a Verb, the sentence is missing a main verb. The phrase 'where the lower ends of the tibia and fibula slot neatly around the talus' acts as a clause describing the ankle joint, but the main verb for the subject 'ankle joint' is absent. 'Occur' is the correct verb to complete the sentence.

Frasa 'where the lower ends of the tibia and fibula slot neatly around the talus' berfungsi sebagai klausa yang menjelaskan 'ankle joint', tetapi kata kerja utama untuk subjek 'ankle joint' tidak ada. 'Occur' adalah kata kerja yang tepat untuk melengkapi kalimat.

reason.en: The word 'lower' is an adjective modifying 'ends'. The sentence already has a subject ('ankle joint') and needs a verb, not another adjective.
reason.id: Kata 'lower' adalah kata sifat yang memodifikasi 'ends'. Kalimat tersebut sudah memiliki subjek ('ankle joint') dan membutuhkan kata kerja, bukan kata sifat lain.

reason.en: The word 'neatly' is an adverb modifying the verb 'slot'. While grammatically correct in its position, it does not fulfill the sentence's primary need for a main verb.
reason.id: Kata 'neatly' adalah kata keterangan yang memodifikasi kata kerja 'slot'. Meskipun secara tata bahasa benar pada posisinya, kata ini tidak memenuhi kebutuhan utama kalimat akan kata kerja utama.

reason.en: The word 'around' is a preposition. The sentence structure requires a main verb to connect the subject 'ankle joint' to the descriptive clause, not another preposition.
reason.id: Kata 'around' adalah kata depan (preposisi). Struktur kalimat membutuhkan kata kerja utama untuk menghubungkan subjek 'ankle joint' dengan klausa deskriptif, bukan kata depan lain.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION TWO — STRUCTURE & WRITTEN EXPRESSION - Skill 1 — Be Sure the Sentence Has a Subject and a Verb

questions.question: Q: In the United States and Canada, ~[motor vehicle]~(A) laws ~[affect]~(B) the ~[operate]~(C) of motorcycles ~[as well as]~(D) automobiles.

reason.en: The word 'motor vehicle' is a noun and is grammatically correct in this context. It functions as part of the subject phrase.
reason.id: Kata 'motor vehicle' adalah kata benda dan secara tata bahasa sudah benar dalam konteks ini. Kata ini berfungsi sebagai bagian dari frasa subjek.

reason.en: The word 'affect' is a verb and is grammatically correct in this context. It fits the sentence structure.
reason.id: Kata 'affect' adalah kata kerja dan secara tata bahasa sudah benar dalam konteks ini. Kata ini sesuai dengan struktur kalimat.

reason.en: Based on Structure Skill 30-32 (Form of the Verb), the sentence requires a verb in its base form after the modal verb 'operate'. The word 'operate' is a verb, but the sentence structure implies a need for a noun or gerund to function as the object of the preposition 'the'. The correct form should be 'operation' or similar, making 'operate' the incorrect word choice.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Structure Skill 30-32 (Form of the Verb), kalimat ini membutuhkan kata kerja dalam bentuk dasarnya setelah kata kerja modal 'operate'. Kata 'operate' adalah kata kerja, tetapi struktur kalimat menyiratkan kebutuhan akan kata benda atau gerund untuk berfungsi sebagai objek dari kata depan 'the'. Bentuk yang benar seharusnya adalah 'operation' atau yang serupa, menjadikan 'operate' pilihan kata yang salah.

reason.en: The phrase 'as well as' is a conjunction and is grammatically correct in this context, connecting two parts of the sentence.
reason.id: Frasa 'as well as' adalah konjungsi dan secara tata bahasa sudah benar dalam konteks ini, menghubungkan dua bagian kalimat.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION TWO — STRUCTURE & WRITTEN EXPRESSION - Skills 30–32 - Form of the Verb

questions.question: Q: The neocortex ~[is]~(A), in evolutionary ~[terms]~(B), ~[most]~(C) recent ~[layer]~(D) of the brain.

reason.en: The word 'is' is the correct verb to agree with the singular subject 'neocortex'. Replacing it would make the sentence grammatically incorrect.
reason.id: Kata 'is' adalah kata kerja yang tepat untuk subjek tunggal 'neocortex'. Menggantinya akan membuat kalimat tidak gramatikal.

reason.en: The word 'terms' is a noun and fits grammatically in the phrase 'in evolutionary terms'. Replacing it would disrupt the idiomatic expression and the sentence structure.
reason.id: Kata 'terms' adalah kata benda dan secara gramatikal cocok dalam frasa 'in evolutionary terms'. Menggantinya akan mengganggu ekspresi idiomatik dan struktur kalimat.

reason.en: Based on Structure Skill 27: Comparatives and Superlatives, the word 'most' is correctly used here as a superlative modifier for 'recent' to indicate that the neocortex is the latest in a series of developments. The sentence compares the neocortex to other evolutionary developments of the brain, implying a comparison among more than two stages, thus requiring a superlative.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Structure Skill 27: Comparatives and Superlatives, kata 'most' digunakan dengan benar di sini sebagai pengubah superlatif untuk 'recent' untuk menunjukkan bahwa neokorteks adalah yang terbaru dalam serangkaian perkembangan. Kalimat ini membandingkan neokorteks dengan perkembangan evolusioner otak lainnya, menyiratkan perbandingan di antara lebih dari dua tahapan, sehingga memerlukan bentuk superlatif.

reason.en: The word 'layer' is a noun that correctly functions as the object of the superlative phrase 'most recent'. Replacing it would make the sentence incomplete and nonsensical.
reason.id: Kata 'layer' adalah kata benda yang berfungsi dengan benar sebagai objek dari frasa superlatif 'most recent'. Menggantinya akan membuat kalimat tidak lengkap dan tidak masuk akal.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION TWO — STRUCTURE & WRITTEN EXPRESSION - Skill 27: Comparatives and Superlatives

questions.question: Q: There ~[are]~(A) more than eighty-four million ~[specimens]~(B) in the National Museum of Natural History's ~[collection]~(C) of biological, geological, archeological, and ~[anthropology]~(D) treasures.

The correct form is 'anthropological'.

Bentuk yang benar adalah 'anthropological'.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION TWO — STRUCTURE & WRITTEN EXPRESSION - Skills 39–42 — Nouns

questions.question: Q: ~[After]~(A) George Washington married ~[widow]~(B) Martha Custis, the couple ~[came]~(C) to ~[resides]~(D) at Mount Vernon.

reason.en: The word 'After' is a preposition that correctly introduces a time clause. It does not contain a grammatical error.
reason.id: Kata 'After' adalah preposisi yang memperkenalkan klausa waktu dengan benar. Kata ini tidak mengandung kesalahan tata bahasa.

reason.en: The word 'widow' is a noun and is used correctly in this context to refer to Martha Custis. It does not contain a grammatical error.
reason.id: Kata 'widow' adalah kata benda dan digunakan dengan benar dalam konteks ini untuk merujuk pada Martha Custis. Kata ini tidak mengandung kesalahan tata bahasa.

reason.en: The word 'came' is the past tense of 'come' and is grammatically correct in this sentence, which describes a past event.
reason.id: Kata 'came' adalah bentuk lampau dari 'come' dan secara tata bahasa benar dalam kalimat ini, yang menggambarkan peristiwa di masa lalu.

reason.en: Based on Structure Skill 30-32 (Form of the Verb), the sentence describes a past event ('came'). Therefore, the verb describing the action of residing should also be in the past tense. 'Resides' is present tense and is incorrect. The correct form would be 'resided'.

Oleh karena itu, kata kerja yang menggambarkan tindakan tinggal ('reside') seharusnya juga dalam bentuk lampau. 'Resides' adalah bentuk sekarang (present tense) dan salah. Bentuk yang benar seharusnya adalah 'resided'.

questions.question: Q: At this stage in ~[their]~(A) development, ~[rubberized]~(B) asphalt can ~[hardly]~(C) be classified ~[as]~(D) cutting edge.

reason.en: The word 'rubberized' is an adjective modifying 'asphalt'. It is correctly used here and does not represent an error in pronoun usage.
reason.id: Kata 'rubberized' adalah kata sifat yang memodifikasi 'asphalt'. Kata ini digunakan dengan benar di sini dan tidak mewakili kesalahan dalam penggunaan kata ganti.

reason.id: Kata 'hardly' adalah kata keterangan yang memodifikasi frasa kata kerja 'can be classified' dengan benar. Kata ini menunjukkan tingkat kemungkinan yang rendah dan secara tata bahasa benar dalam konteks ini.

reason.en: The word 'as' is a preposition used correctly here to indicate classification or role. It is not a pronoun and therefore not the source of the error.
reason.id: Kata 'as' adalah preposisi yang digunakan dengan benar di sini untuk menunjukkan klasifikasi atau peran. Kata ini bukan kata ganti dan oleh karena itu bukan sumber kesalahan.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION TWO — STRUCTURE & WRITTEN EXPRESSION - Skills 43–45 — Pronouns

questions.question: Q: Rhesus monkeys ~[exhibit]~(A) patterns of ~[shy]~(B) similar ~[to]~(C) ~[those]~(D) in humans.

reason.en: The word 'exhibit' is a verb. The sentence already has a verb ('exhibit'). The sentence requires an adjective to describe the patterns, not another verb. Therefore, 'exhibit' is not the correct choice.
reason.id: Kata 'exhibit' adalah kata kerja. Kalimat tersebut sudah memiliki kata kerja ('exhibit'). Kalimat tersebut membutuhkan kata sifat untuk mendeskripsikan pola, bukan kata kerja lain. Oleh karena itu, 'exhibit' bukanlah pilihan yang tepat.

reason.en: Based on Structure & Written Expression Skill 46-51 (Adjectives and Adverbs), the word 'shy' is an adjective. The sentence needs an adjective to describe the patterns of behavior. 'Shy' fits grammatically and semantically, describing a characteristic that can be observed in both rhesus monkeys and humans.
reason.id: Berdasarkan Structure & Written Expression Skill 46-51 (Adjectives and Adverbs), kata 'shy' adalah kata sifat. Kalimat tersebut membutuhkan kata sifat untuk mendeskripsikan pola perilaku. 'Shy' cocok secara tata bahasa dan makna, menggambarkan karakteristik yang dapat diamati pada monyet rhesus dan manusia.

reason.en: The word 'to' is a preposition. While prepositions are important in sentence structure, the sentence requires an adjective to modify 'patterns' and describe their similarity to human patterns. 'To' does not fulfill this grammatical role.
